Ad Text:
At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That's why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members. We are dedicated to building a high-performance environment underpinned by a strong risk culture, ensuring we deliver the best outcomes for our members.


Your New Role

In this 12-month contract you will be a key contributor to the execution of remediation activities that allows appropriate and sustainable remediation outcomes for members and the Fund. The methods, approaches and oversight must support the Fund's compliance with RG277 and other regulatory obligations including the Superannuation Industry Supervision Act (SIS Act), and other relevant legislation.

You will be responsible for restoring Member's back to the position they would have been in had an error not occurred. This includes contributing through both independent and collaborative thinking to the design, development & delivery of innovative solutions in line with the remediation lifecycle as detailed below.

To be successful in this role you will have experience in superannuation operations, along with advanced analytical and assurance capabilities. You will be responsible for using your BA expertise to capture, manage, and communicate remediation requirements across stakeholders on multiple large, complex initiatives simultaneously.

Key duties include but not limited to;

Analyse incidents to form a view on scoping, methodology, member communication and payment approaches.
Data analysis, QA and validation to ensure all impacted members are put in the position they should have been if the error had not occurred
Assist in defining and documenting remediation and compensation methodologies for decision to evidence our requirements under RG277 and results in remediating our Members honestly, efficiently and fairly. Independent quality assurance of complex ongoing projects, without prior knowledge or direct involvement.
Validate the accuracy and completeness of incident records, transaction histories, and member data to ensure remediation outcomes are correct.
Flexibility to switch between various project environments such as Waterfall, Agile and a hybrid of both dependent on the level of uncertainty. Leveraging technology to improve execution capabilities, quality, and operational efficiency.
Execution of remediation activities including data analysis, delivery of payments and communication runs and quality assurance activities.
Ensure all data inputs and outputs are traceable and auditable, supporting compliance with regulatory requirements such as RG277
Implement strong risk controls on remediation execution processes


What You'll Need

3+ years' experience in superannuation operations.
Clear understanding of industry remediation guidance, including RG 277.
Demonstrates robust analytical and problem-solving skills.
Demonstrated capacity to manage and contribute to several projects concurrently.
Demonstrated ability to collaborate and to be flexible and adaptable.
Skilled in Excel, Word and PowerPoint.
Possess basic SQL skills to query and analyse large datasets.
Experience working on Remediation initiatives and managing these from beginning to end. This includes being responsible for completing remediation execution activities including data analysis, Payment and Communication runs and quality assurance activities.


Life at AustralianSuper

AustralianSuper is committed to colleague development, and we support our people with ongoing learning, coaching and training, as well as career opportunities across our expanding global organisation. We offer generous leave entitlements and promote a blended working environment in which all roles can flex, and we're happy to discuss what this looks like for you.

We cultivate a workplace that champions safety, respect, inclusiveness and diversity. We are committed to supporting our diverse workforce in a way that is inclusive and embraces diversity in all its forms. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process or the role, please let our recruitment team know.

What's Next

Apply now, if you share our values of Energy, Integrity, Generosity of Spirit and Excellent Outcomes and would like the opportunity to work in a challenging, growing and rapidly evolving team to deliver outstanding results.

Australian or New Zealand citizenship or Australian permanent residency status is required.

Progress powered by purpose.

https://www.australiansuper.com/careers/candidate-privacy-notice

Agencies please note: this vacancy is being managed directly by AustralianSuper's Talent Acquisition team. We will contact our preferred agency partners should we require additional support. Thank you.

The Role:
 
I'm currently looking for a heavy-hitting Senior Business Analyst who specialises in Process Engineering to join a fast-paced financial services firm. This isn't your typical "sit in the corner and write requirements" BA role. This is a high-visibility, high-impact position designed for someone who wants to take a "blank sheet" environment and build a gold-standard operational framework.
 
The business is growing rapidly, but the documentation hasn't kept pace. We need a self-starter who can walk into various divisions, identify the "tribal knowledge" keeping things afloat, and formalise it into streamlined, efficient workflows.
What you'll actually be doing

Process Mapping from Scratch: Since there isn't much currently documented, you'll be leading the charge in discovering, documenting, and "owning" the end-to-end business processes.
Process Engineering & Optimisation: Using Lean, Six Sigma, or similar methodologies to identify bottlenecks and redundant steps. You won't just document the "As-Is"—you will design and pitch the "To-Be."
Challenging the Status Quo: You'll need to "ruffle a few feathers." We need someone who isn't afraid to look a Department Head in the eye and explain why their 10-year-old manual process is holding the business back.
Driving Efficiency: Your success will be measured by tangible outputs: reduced turnaround times, lower error rates, and better resource allocation across the firm.
Advisory & Influence: Serving as a subject matter expert to the executive team on how we can use process automation and better workflow design to scale the business.
Who you are

A Process Specialist: You likely come from a Process Engineering or heavy Operational Design background. You think in flows, logic gates, and efficiency metrics.
FS Experience: You understand the constraints of a regulated environment. You know how to balance "speed and efficiency" with "compliance and audit trails."
Resilient & Assertive: You're comfortable with ambiguity and even more comfortable with resistance. You have the soft skills to manage stakeholders, but the backbone to push through necessary changes.
Why this role is different

Visibility: You'll be reporting into senior leadership; your work will be the blueprint for how this company operates for the next five years.
Autonomy: No micro-management here. You'll be expected to set your own agenda and decide which areas of the business need the most urgent "surgery."
The Challenge: If you hate red tape and love building things from the ground up, this is the perfect project.
The Essentials

Significant experience as a Senior BA or Process Engineer within Financial Services (Insurance, Banking, or Wealth Management).
Proven track record of streamlining workflows that resulted in measurable ROI or efficiency gains.
A "hunter" mindset for finding inefficiencies—you don't wait to be told where the problems are.
Exceptional communication skills; you can simplify complex technical processes for any audience.

Head of Quantitative
B2B Financial Services Technology | Global Scale | Sydney (Hybrid)

Greenfield opportunity to define a global quantitative capability


Direct influence on product strategy and international expansion


Work with sophisticated institutional clients at scale


A high-growth B2B financial services technology company is appointing a Head of Quantitative in a newly created role to build and lead its next-generation modelling capability.

Operating at the intersection of wealth management, actuarial science and enterprise software, this business partners with tier-one financial institutions across Australia, the UK and Europe to modernise how financial advice is delivered at scale.

This is a rare opportunity to architect stochastic modelling frameworks from first principles, while building and leading a world-class quantitative function with global reach.

The opportunity
The business is at a pivotal inflection point and is poised for continued market traction.

Having successfully deployed deterministic advice models at institutional scale, the next phase of growth requires the introduction of sophisticated stochastic modelling to unlock new markets, new products and materially improved customer outcomes.

As Head of Quantitative, you will:

Define the quantitative strategy


Lead the technical uplift to stochastic modelling


Build and scale a high-performing team


Act as the ultimate authority on model risk, integrity and regulatory alignment
This is a genuine player–coach role, with significant hands-on technical ownership.

What you’ll be responsible for
Architect and deliver stochastic modelling frameworks (Monte Carlo, scenario generation, multi-goal optimisation)


Lead the transition from deterministic Excel-based models to scalable calculation engines


Translate complex regulatory and advice policy requirements into robust quantitative logic


Build, mentor and scale a quantitative team across Australia and global markets


Partner closely with Product and Engineering to productionise models at scale


Engage directly with tier-one institutional clients, actuarial teams and regulators


Own quantitative governance, validation and model risk standards globally
What it will take to make an impact
Deep expertise in stochastic / actuarial / quantitative modelling


Strong hands-on background in tools such as Excel, Python or actuarial modelling platforms


Experience operating in regulated financial services environments (wealth, retirement, insurance or advice technology)


Proven ability to lead technical teams while remaining hands-on


Confidence engaging with C-level stakeholders, institutional clients and regulators


Comfort operating in ambiguity within a high-growth, product-led business


Advanced qualifications in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics or a related quantitative field are highly regarded.

If you’re motivated by complex problems, real-world impact and global scale, this is a rare leadership opportunity.  Call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset on 0413 463 043 for a confidential discussion or apply online.

We are recruiting for a strategic and hands-on Business Analysis Manager to transform a current BA function into a high-performing, multi-functional practice. The existing practice is in its early stages, and they need a leader who can move beyond "just gathering requirements" to establish a robust framework that adds tangible value across the entire project portfolio.
 
In this role, you won't just be managing people—you will be the architect of our BA methodology. You will refine our processes, elevate our standards, and grow a team capable of supporting everything from regulatory compliance and risk management to digital transformation and customer experience initiatives.
 
Key Responsibilities
 
1. Practice Leadership & Strategy

Methodology Design: Develop and implement a standardised BA toolkit (e.g., requirements traceability, process mapping, user stories, and acceptance criteria) tailored for a regulated financial environment.
Ways of Working: Shift the practice from a reactive state to a proactive partner by integrating BA activities early in the project lifecycle.
Quality Assurance: Establish Peer Review processes and KPIs to ensure all BA deliverables are accurate, consistent, and of high quality.
2. Team Management & Development

Leadership: Directly manage a core team of Business Analysts, providing mentorship, coaching, and performance management.
Capability Building: Conduct gap analyses on current team skills and implement a training roadmap to build a "multi-functional" team (e.g., bridging the gap between technical, data, and business BAs).
Resourcing: Manage demand planning and resource allocation across various workstreams, ensuring the right BAs are matched to the right projects.
3. Delivery & Stakeholder Engagement

Business Partnership: Act as the primary point of contact for senior stakeholders to understand business strategy and translate it into actionable change requirements.
Change Advocacy: Promote the value of professional Business Analysis across the organisation, influencing headers of departments to adopt new frameworks.
Complex Problem Solving: Step in to lead analysis on high-profile, high-risk, or particularly complex initiatives when required.
Your background: 

Financial Services Pedigree: You understand the nuances of a regulated environment—you know that "impact analysis" includes considering risk, compliance, and reporting requirements.
The "Builder" Mindset: You have a proven track record of evolving an immature function. You enjoy the challenge of creating order out of ambiguity and don't mind getting "stuck in" to get things moving.
Methodology Expertise: Deep knowledge of diverse delivery frameworks (Agile, Waterfall, and Hybrid).
Influencing Skills: You can navigate a corporate environment and convince stakeholders of the long-term benefits of investing in rigorous analysis.

Together, we can do great things

We shape leading experiences across office, retail, logistics and student accommodation.

We're one of Australia's largest property groups, managing $40 billion worth of assets across the country.

Our sustainable returns from investments come from focusing on what matters most to customers — a great experience and doing good. Every day, our people are driving positive impact for people, place and planet. They're inquisitive, pioneering industry firsts and pushing new ideas to deliver exceptional outcomes.

This passion and expertise creates an employee experience that puts wellbeing, flexibility and opportunity first.

Come join us.

The role

Due to growth and volume of work, we are currently seeking a Fund Analyst to join GPT's Wholesale Shopping Centre Fund (GWSCF) team.

This role forms an integral part of the GWSCF team, working closely senior members, to ensure the Fund meets investor and management requirements, executes on strategic objectives and optimises investment returns.

The role will also critically contribute to the analysis, modelling and reporting of investment performance for the Fund.

What you will be doing

Attending to investors' queries in a timely and accurate manner and maintain investor information calendar
Preparing and coordinating information, communication and associated due diligence for investor updates, Board proposals and reports, quarterly, annual and half yearly reports
Preparing presentations and materials required for investor engagement and for the Fund's contribution to GPT results.
Assisting in the creation and preparation of financial models and scenario analysis;
Supporting the Fund Manager to prepare investment recommendations to investment committees and investors.
Working with the GWSCF team to ensure that the investment process is truly research driven, drawing upon insights from the global and domestic market, to assist the Fund Manager to formulate a strategy for the portfolio which guides future investment decisions.

What makes you stand out

We are seeking a Fund Analyst with a degree in economics, finance, property or a related discipline with proficiency in Excel and strong communication skills both in written and verbal form.

This role requires someone who has well developed numeric and financial analysis skills, and can interpret data sets, identify meaningful insights and trends and articulate these findings in a clear and actionable.

This role would suit a pro-active Fund Analyst who takes accountability for delivering accurate and timely outputs, with a strong desire to learn and develop professionally and wishes to grow their career within a leading property investment company.

What makes us stand out

We value our people

Enjoy flexible working arrangements and generous leave options.
Experience market-leading parental leave benefits including 22 weeks paid leave for either carer and childcare allowance once you return to work.
Embrace diverse and challenging career opportunities, supported by our dedicated Learning & Development Team committed to your growth.
Engage in comprehensive Wellbeing programs and benefit from market-leading policies and support tailored to diverse needs.
Be recognised by peers through nomination awards and take part in our generous Employee Referral Program.

We know the power of a great experience

Ranked 5th in the 2025 Gender Equality Global Report released by Equileap.
Placed 1st of real estate investment trusts in the S&P Global Corporate Sustainability Assessment 2025 Yearbook.
Earned a Gold ranking in Australian Workplace Equality Index (AWEI) for LGBTQIA+ inclusion.
Awarded our fifth consecutive Employer of Choice for Gender Equality citation from the Workplace Gender Equality Agency (WGEA) in 2023-2025.
Accredited Family Inclusive Workplace™.
Committed to our second Stretch Reconciliation Action Plan (RAP).

Everyone counts

GPT offers an inclusive workplace culture and embraces people with diverse perspectives, skills and backgrounds. We welcome applications from First Nations candidates, members of the LGBTQIA+ community, people living with disability and people of diverse ages, cultures and backgrounds.

Let's get started

If this role sounds like you, we want to hear from you. Go for it!

Click on "Apply" to submit your application and Mandy from the Talent Acquisition Team will be in touch.

Location: South West & M5 Corridor, Sydney NSW (Hybrid)
Ad Text:
Rare opportunity for a highly proactive Program Analyst to step into a central role and influence high-level decision making. Apply now!

Key role supporting the delivery of complex, high-profile projects

One-off opportunity to work with a respected organisation

Bring coherence to complex portfolio data by translating it into actionable insights




Your New Role:

We’re seeking a highly proactive Program Analyst to support a diverse portfolio of complex, large-scale and high-profile projects. In this central role, you will bring together data from across the portfolio, turning it into clear, actionable insights that inform decision-making for senior stakeholders and steering committees.

You will:

Consolidate data to deliver a clear, integrated view of portfolio performance

Produce high-quality management reports and presentations

Support governance processes, ensuring compliance with established frameworks and regulatory requirements

Build strong relationships with project stakeholders to gather insights, understand challenges, and close information gaps

Analyse data to identify emerging risks and pressure points, and translating findings into practical recommendations

Coordinate with stakeholders to monitor costs, refine plans, and ensure alignment with overall strategy


Your Background:

Degree qualifications in Business Administration, Project Management or a related discipline

Demonstrated experience in a similar position supporting successful delivery of projects or programs within a complex environment

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with the ability to build trusted working relationships across diverse teams

Highly proactive, organised and detail-oriented, with the ability to manage competing priorities and deadlines

Australian Permanent Resident or Australian Citizen


The Company

Our client is a world-renowned organisation undertaking extremely interesting and innovative industrial projects. The organisation boasts long term employees and provides a great working environment, where your skills will be well utilised and continue to develop. It is a fabulous environment for learning and career growth as well as work/life balance.




This is an exceptional opportunity to enhance your career and be valued for your contributions. These types of opportunities don't come along that often. Don't miss out! APPLY NOW! Please submit your application using a MS Word compatible document.

What's in it for you?
This is an opportunity to lead the design and execution of a comprehensive Finance Intelligence Program at a global scale. You will have the technical ownership to integrate data, automation, and AI capabilities into our core financial operations. Working at the intersection of Finance and Tech, you will be responsible for transforming raw data into secure, scalable, and efficient financial processes that directly impact decision-making for some of the largest brands in the iGaming industry.

Your role with us:
As the Finance Transformation Manager, you will be the primary architect of our finance data ecosystem. This role requires a strong technical background in data management and architecture, as you will be responsible for defining data schemes and ensuring the integrity of complex datasets. Expertise in Jira Service Management (JSM) is a core requirement, as you will design the automation workflows across Finance, HR, Legal, and IT, ensuring our ERP and operational systems remain a "single source of truth.

Who are we?
Easygo is the Australian powerhouse behind some of the most popular brands within the iGaming industry, including Stake, KICK and Twist Gaming. 

Stake is the world's most popular online casino, and leads the industry with a seamless online casino and sportsbook experience. Level up your online entertainment with KICK, the vibrant live streaming platform, which connects millions of gamers and content creators worldwide. All alongside the innovative game design studio, Twist Gaming, which takes creativity to new heights by crafting cutting-edge and captivating games.

Headquartered in the beautiful city of Melbourne, our growth has been remarkable. From humble beginnings to a thriving workforce of 600+, we've expanded not only in numbers but in ambition. There really is something for everyone here, whether you work in Tech, Marketing, Operations, Mathematics or Design, we are sure to have something for everyone.

Click play, on your career today!

What you will do:

Design and oversee the finance data architecture, including development data schemes covering transactions, payments, taxes and other operation metrics, ensuring consistency, traceability, and alignment across all finance systems.
Lead the integration of data from ERP systems, trading/payment platforms, gateways, and operational tools, establishing automated ingestion pipelines that standardize, reconcile, and maintain high-quality financial data within the data cloud.
Establish robust data governance frameworks, including validation rules, reconciliation controls, audit trails, and access management, ensuring financial data integrity, compliance with policies, and readiness for audit and regulatory requirements.
Design and implement end-to-end automation workflows on JSM based on policies across Finance, HR, Legal, and IT, ensuring synchronized data assets across ERP and other operational systems.
Lead the implementation of automated reporting solutions, including standardised financial and management dashboards for various purpose ensuring consistent, accurate, and timely insights for stakeholders.
Explore, evaluate, and implement AI tools to enhance finance processes, embedding intelligent capabilities such as anomaly detection, classification, and predictive insights, while ensuring strong governance, security, and compliance frameworks.
Lead cross-functional collaboration and team enablement by training finance staff on data, automation, and AI tools, while identifying continuous improvement opportunities and ensuring scalable, controlled, and compliant process design.

What you will bring:

5+ years in finance transformation, finance systems, or data/automation/AI initiatives
Strong experience across finance operations (GL, AP, reconciliation, reporting, treasury)
Proven track record delivering cross-functional transformation programs
Strong understanding of finance processes, including transaction flows, reconciliation, reporting, and close processes within multi-entity and multi-system environments
Proficiency in data architecture, data modelling, and data pipeline concepts within cloud-based data environments
Expert in the JSM or other workflow automation and API tool implementation
Familiarity with AI tools and their application in finance, including intelligent automation, document processing, and data-driven decision support
Strong knowledge of data governance, security, and compliance principles, particularly for financial and sensitive data environments
Excellent stakeholder management and communication skills, with the ability to bridge finance, technology, and data teams effectively

Bonus points if you also have:

Finance & IT Management knowledge advantageous
Proficient with Google Suite (Drive, Spreadsheets, Docs), Slack


Some of the perks of working with us:

Office Perks & Environment

In-house baristas serving free coffee, fresh juices and smoothies
Daily catered breakfast and regular company-wide events
Snack walls and drink fridges on every floor
Fun /modern office spaces with pool tables, table tennis, gaming consoles, and an F1 simulator

Wellbeing & Personal Development

Access to our Employee Assistance Program for you and your loved ones
20,000+ courses on our Learning & Development platform
One paid volunteer day per year
Weekly Wednesday massages by professional masseuses

Team Connection & Rewards

Team budgets for lunches and activities to celebrate achievements
Social sports teams and participation in Corporate Games
Easygo branded swag
Birthday and work anniversary gift vouchers, plus a chance to win prizes 

Events & Experiences

Company-wide talks with key partners such as Everton FC and Team Sauber in Formula 1
Office visits from big-name streamers
Meet Ambassadors like Alex Pereira, Israel Adesanya
Ballots for exclusive tickets to events like Formula 1, UFC, and more sporting and music events

We believe that the unique contributions of everyone at Easygo are the driver of our success. To make sure that our products and culture continue to incorporate everyone's perspectives and experiences, we never discriminate on the basis of race, religion, nationality, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, age, or marital, veteran, or disability status. 

We’re a global team of dreamers, creators, and game-changers from every walk of life, united by a passion for entertainment that inspires the world.

We believe that the best ideas come from different perspectives, so we actively welcome and champion talent from all backgrounds, cultures, identities, and experiences. Whether you're just starting out or bringing decades of experience, your unique voice matters here.

Come as you are. Be part of something bigger. Click play on your career today.
